name: Preferred
description_short: Michigan-based credit union offering checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and credit cards with six branch locations and nationwide ATM/branch access.
description_long: Preferred Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution headquartered in Michigan serving members across six physical branch locations in Belding, Grand Rapids, Greenville, Hastings, Kalamazoo, and Ludington. The credit union has been providing traditional banking services to its membership base with a focus on personalized member service and community presence. Preferred offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including deposit accounts (checking and savings), personal and auto loans, home mortgages, home equity lines of credit, credit cards, and related banking services. The institution prioritizes member security with fraud alerts and a dedicated member contact line, demonstrating awareness of modern cybersecurity threats. What distinguishes Preferred is its participation in nationwide networks providing members access to 30,000+ ATMs and 5,000 branch locations through shared branching and co-op arrangements, effectively extending service reach far beyond its six physical locations. The credit union maintains extended call center hours through Saturday and offers multiple service channels including drive-thru, night drop, and appointment-based Saturday lobby access at select locations. Members can apply for loans, mortgages, and credit products online or through branches, and the institution provides resources for rate information and membership education. As a credit union, Preferred operates on a not-for-profit member-owned model rather than a shareholder model, theoretically aligning with member interests. However, the website provides limited transparency on specific rates, fees, membership eligibility requirements, or competitive positioning versus other Michigan credit unions or regional banks. The institution does not disclose asset size, membership numbers, or detailed product terms, making it difficult for prospective members to evaluate value proposition comprehensively.
pros: Access to 30,000+ ATMs and 5,000+ branch locations nationwide through shared branching network
Six physical branch locations across Michigan with drive-thru, night drop, and ATM services
Extended call center hours including Saturday (8am-5:30pm weekdays, 9am-12pm Saturday)
Member-owned credit union structure operates on not-for-profit basis
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, loans, mortgages, home equity, and credit cards
Proactive member security alerts and fraud prevention messaging on website
Nationwide shared branching allows deposits, withdrawals, loan payments, and fund transfers at 5,000+ locations
cons: Limited branch network compared to regional banks—only 6 locations across Michigan
Website provides no specific APRs, interest rates, or fee schedules for any products
No transparent membership eligibility criteria or field of membership information disclosed
Limited online product details—must call or visit branches for rates and terms
Shared branching restrictions noted (some locations charge fees, daily cash withdrawal limits may apply)
best_for: Michigan residents seeking community-based credit union banking with local branch access
Borrowers needing mortgage or home equity financing through a member-owned institution
Members traveling nationally who want fee-free ATM and branch access through shared branching networks
diagnosis: Preferred Credit Union is best for Michigan residents and credit union members seeking traditional banking services, mortgages, or loans with local branch presence and nationwide ATM/shared branching access. The main caveat is that the website lacks competitive rate and fee transparency, requiring prospective members to contact the institution directly to compare terms.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
